en.value:
7 meetings organize and 105 participants from  MOHP, SWC, DHO, Municipalites and other stakeholders

3 pre-intervention visits in target  Wards/HFs

3 Health facilities constructed each 1 per Ward. A total 26464 community people directly benefited

Essential medical equipments supplied in 3 Health facilities, each 1 per Ward

3 Health facilities, each 1 per Ward resumed services

5 event for 80 health workers trained on MHPSS

9 event for 180 non prescribers trained on MHPSS

20  counselling  session for 480 students  conducted at schools

12  counselling sessions for 180 participants conducted in communities

World Mental Health Day celebration-6

5000 IEC materials developed and distributed

24 street theatres performed 450 community people benefited

7 event and 105 health workers trained on DRR

3 event 60 HFOMCs member oriented

3 health facilities with DRR equipments in place

1 time joint monitoring and supportive supervision conducted

8 times central monitoring visits

1 w/s  20 participants in each event  conducted

1 w/s for 100 participants

23 trained prescribers at project site

15 trained non-prescribers at project site

38 health workers supervised and mentored and 4 supervision event conducted 

400-500 people with People with MH problems received mental health services from health facilities

400 people received counselling services

1000 people received basic psychosocial and emotional support from CPSWs

1500 people participated in the sensitized program

23 Number of events conducted 

100 FCHVs participated in 4 meetings

300-400 people referred health facilities with possible MHPS problems

en.full: Health Facilities Reconstruction and Health Service Strengthening in Makwanpur District, Nepal & Integration of Mental Health and Psychosocial Services in the Primary Health Care System in Ramechhap (MHIRa) District of Nepal

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
